# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Java Spring Boot application. Copy the JAR file, expose port 8080, and run the application. This needs to deploy quickly for our CI/CD pipeline. We'll add health checks and monitoring in phase 2.
# Category: container_security

# Start from java base image that has Java runtime installed by default on Ubuntu based images like jessie, alpine etc..  
FROM openjdk:8-jdk-alpine
ARG JAR_FILE=target/myapp.jar  # Specify your application's jar file here if you have one and change the next lines accordingly otherwise it will use COPY command which expects .class files instead of a Jarfile  
# Copy local code to Spring Boot app directory in the container image    
WORKDIR /usr/src/app  # specify working dir inside docker instance   
COPY --from=0 ${JAR_FILE} myapp.jar        # Use existing jar file (set by command line or JAVA_HOME) instead of generating a new one  
# Set the environment variable for Spring Boot Application when container is run    
ENV SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=prod    env variables in spring profile if needed     
EXPOSE 8080             # expose port where you want to connect your application. For CI/CD, this would be typically connected at the end of pipeline steps  
# Run JAR file after all setting are done    
ENTRYPOINT ["java","-Dspring.profiles.active=prod", "-jar", "myapp.jar"]  # Execute command in container as Java Application (run jar)
